title: convolvulus sandwicensis
intro: Convolvulus sandwicensis, also known as Ma'o hau hele, is a flowering plant native to Hawaii, belonging to the morning glory family (Convolvulaceae). It has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, offering benefits such as wound healing, antimicrobial protection, and reduced inflammation. Convolvulus sandwicensis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile herbal remedy that has been passed down through generations in Hawaiian culture.
